authoring_instructions: Use work_packet.instruction_text and work_packet.compiled_context for scientific judgment; this authoring contract supplies mechanics only.
Write one bare Transaction JSON object, not a candidate wrapper, at output_locations.candidate_logical_path; write helper files only below output_locations.shadow_logical_root.

Copy every field in transaction_bindings that also appears in transaction_json_schema exactly into the Transaction (binding_schema is contract metadata), including base_revision and created_at; choose only transaction_id, intent, preconditions, changed_facets, operations, evidence_refs, and authority_basis as the route and schemas require.
For each typed entity, put {schema: payload_schema_id, payload: <schema-valid object>} in owner_facet and set every listed empty_facet to an empty object.

Set every new EntityVersion and RelationVersion project_id, privacy, access_compartments, and created_at exactly to the corresponding transaction_bindings values; never rely on privacy or compartment defaults.
Use only output_contract allowed operation, entity, and relation types; satisfy every output cardinality and the exact scientific exit conditions in work_packet.instruction_text.
JSON Schema is necessary but not sufficient: obey output_contract.model_invariants exactly and use the bridge's structured candidate diagnostics for any remaining model-level repair.

Include exactly one route.outcome operation bound to transaction_bindings.route_run_id and transaction_bindings.route_id, with the same privacy and access compartments; candidate_refs must enumerate every exact canonical object produced by the Transaction, including any entity, relation, artifact, blocker, or other schema-permitted reference required by the route validator.
Do not fabricate a human decision or approval; obey every work_packet.forbidden_actions entry and stop if the route requires unavailable human authority.

The candidate source may be ordinary readable UTF-8 JSON and may end with a newline. The bridge validates it as a strict Transaction and computes the digest from engine-canonical Transaction bytes; do not hash the source file bytes or put a digest inside the object.

For packet compiler v2, copy output_contract.required_entity_evidence_refs exactly, in order, into Transaction.evidence_refs as EntityVersionRefs. Preconditions and route.outcome candidate_refs do not substitute for Transaction.evidence_refs; they have different meanings.

model_invariants.repair_hint: Use trace_only for provenance links; relation importance alone does not justify hard invalidation.
model_invariants.requirement: upstream and downstream must both be null

model_invariants.repair_hint: Either add both exact facet endpoints or change a provenance-only link to trace_only.
model_invariants.requirement: upstream must bind the exact source entity/version and downstream must bind the exact target entity/version

model_invariants.repair_hint: Choose exact scope equality or typed overlap evidence, but never both.
model_invariants.requirement: exactly one of scope_ref or scope_overlap is required; other modes must omit scope_overlap

model_invariants.repair_hint: Bind the exact immediately preceding immutable relation version.
model_invariants.requirement: version 1 omits supersedes; version n > 1 supersedes version n - 1 with the same relation_id

transaction_json_schema.description: Canonical transaction body.  Its digest is intentionally absent.
transaction_json_schema.title: Transaction

exact_primitives: A full-support binary qualification state shared by both regimes.
One fixed informative institutional score with values low, middle, and high, ordered by qualification posterior.
The same fixed initial rule accepts high and rejects low or middle.
The same strictly informative binary private applicant signal arrives after rejection.
The same strictly informative fresh binary review signal and the same fixed final decision rule apply after appeal.
The same positive applicant acceptance value, positive appeal cost, Bayesian beliefs, and tie rule apply.
The institution evaluates the same classification consequences and positive per-appeal processing burden, without a hard capacity or rationing rule.
Under decision_only, a rejected applicant observes only the pooled event that the institutional score was low or middle, then observes the private signal before choosing whether to appeal.
benchmarks.label: Decision-only after rejection
benchmarks.prediction: No directional prediction is imposed. Decision_only supplies the pooled-rejection posterior and its induced appeal set against which the treatment's volume, composition, corrected qualified rejections, and review-induced unqualified acceptances are measured.
benchmarks.solution_concept: Bayesian posterior-threshold appeal choice under the fixed review technology and final rule, with indifference resolved in favor of appeal.
timing: Nature draws qualification and signals under the fixed joint law.
The institution observes the coarse score and accepts high while rejecting low or middle.
A rejected applicant learns only the rejection message under decision_only and then privately observes the binary applicant signal.
The rejected applicant chooses whether to pay the positive appeal cost under a posterior-threshold rule.
If the applicant appeals, the fresh review signal is realized and the fixed final rule is applied; otherwise rejection is final.
benchmarks.unresolved_delta: Determine which positive-probability score-private-signal cells enter or leave appeal when the pooled rejection message is refined into low versus middle, and characterize the resulting outcome-vector changes over open parameter regions.

payload.exact_question_delta: The sole treatment is score_disclosure: after the same rejection, the applicant additionally learns whether the rejected institutional score was low or middle before observing the same private signal and making the same appeal choice. Relative to decision_only, only the rejected applicant's information partition changes. The prior and joint signal law, applicant value and appeal cost, initial acceptance rule, appeal technology, final review rule, institutional classification consequences, and per-appeal processing burden remain fixed. Every delta is oriented score_disclosure minus decision_only and resolves to ResearchQuestion rq_score_disclosure_appeal_selection version 1; no no-appeal, mandatory-appeal, full-score, altered-review, pricing, hard-capacity, or rationing case is a coequal benchmark.

entities.summary: The exact one-benchmark comparison in which score_disclosure changes only the rejected applicant's information partition from pooled rejection to low-versus-middle disclosure.
entities.title: Decision-only benchmark for rejected-score disclosure

payload.importance: The question separates the amount of reconsideration from the selection of cases into reconsideration. A useful characterization would show whether disclosure shifts the institution's classification-processing frontier or only trades one component against another, while keeping applicant incentives separate from the institution's consequences and avoiding an aggregate welfare ranking.
payload.kill_condition: Park or kill this framing if low and middle disclosure does not alter appeal behavior on any nonempty open parameter region, or if all results reduce to a mechanical finite-cell threshold crossing or unrestricted loss weights with no interpretable posterior ordering, selection decomposition, or sufficient statistic. A hard processing-capacity mechanism, congestion, or endogenous review rule would require a later framing decision rather than being silently added here.
payload.object_to_explain: Characterize the primitive signal structures and positive appeal-cost ranges for which score_disclosure, relative to decision_only, changes which rejected score-private-signal types appeal, and map that selection change into the institution's vector of total processing load, qualified appellants, corrected qualified rejections, and review-induced acceptances of unqualified applicants.
payload.phenomenon: After a fixed initial rule rejects both low- and middle-score applicants, disclosing which rejected score occurred refines the applicant's information before a costly appeal. The refinement may move different score-private-signal cells across the appeal threshold and can change appeal volume and appellant composition without a predetermined improvement in classification.
prohibited_claims: Score disclosure necessarily increases or decreases appeal volume.
Score disclosure necessarily improves classification quality or the institution's objective.
The applicant's and institution's objectives admit a fixed aggregate welfare ranking.
A hard capacity, rationing rule, congestion technology, or endogenous review standard is part of the present frame.
The proposed framing is novel, formally validated, empirically supported, policy optimal, or human-approved.
payload.proposed_scope: Static Bayesian application model with a full-support binary qualification state; an informative three-valued institutional score ordered low, middle, high; a fixed initial rule accepting high and rejecting low and middle; a strictly informative binary private post-rejection applicant signal; a strictly informative fresh binary review signal; and a fixed final rule. Begin with score, applicant signal, and review signal conditionally independent given qualification as an explicit maintained simplification, with every relevant score-signal cell positive. The applicant has a common positive acceptance value and positive appeal cost, updates Bayesianly, and appeals when the fixed review-success probability makes appeal weakly worthwhile, with indifference resolved in favor of appeal. The institution separately tracks classification consequences and a positive per-appeal processing burden. This burden represents scarce resources without introducing a hard capacity, rationing, congestion, or endogenous review quality. Truthful committed disclosure, initial participation, all signal laws, the initial rule, the review technology, and the final rule are fixed across regimes; non-appellants remain rejected, and the fixed final rule does not infer the private signal from the act of appealing. No sign, theorem, policy ranking, novelty claim, or validation is proposed.
payload.unresolved_delta: In the smallest nondegenerate Bayesian environment, when does revealing whether a rejected score was low or middle, rather than revealing rejection alone, strictly change the appeal set on a positive-probability cell, and what exact classification-processing frontier change follows when the initial rule, review technology, and final rule are held fixed?

entities.summary: A proposed characterization of when revealing low versus middle after rejection changes the set and composition of appellants and thereby changes the institution's classification and processing outcomes, relative to decision_only.
entities.title: When does rejected-score disclosure change appeal selection?

content.authority: This route may explore and propose at L1. It cannot create human confirmation or alter a confirmed scientific scope.
content.budget_estimator: etai_lexical_v1 is deterministic lexical accounting, not a provider token count.
content.scope: Preserve exact assumptions, quantifiers, scope, dissent, and stale blockers. Generated context and candidates are noncanonical.
content.validity: Formal truth, economic interpretation, literature novelty, human acceptance, and freshness are distinct.

instructions: Decompose the framed environment into economically meaningful primitives, timing, information, actions, payoffs, and solution concept candidates.
Name which primitives differ from each exact benchmark and which are merely normalizations.
Produce a PrimitiveGraph and a G1 GateDossier with importance, kill conditions, scope risks, and exact supporting references.
Do not confirm G1; only a human decision may promote the question and benchmark scope.
Bind the G1 GateDossier to the exact ResearchQuestion ref, include that ref among its ordered objects, and keep every output in that one scope.
